question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: The Atlanta Fed GDPNow model, widely regarded as the most accurate real-time GDP nowcast, has consistently revised Q1 2026 growth estimates downward throughout April. The latest estimate as of April 7th is only 1.3%, down from 1.6% on April 2nd and 2.0% on March 23rd. This significant deterioration in nowcast estimates, coupled with weak Q4 2025 growth of only 1.4% (later revised down to 0.7%), suggests economic momentum has slowed considerably. With the BEA advance estimate due April 30th, the probability of exceeding 2.0% appears much lower than the 54% market price suggests.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow estimate declined to 1.3% as of April 7, 2026
Consistent downward revisions throughout March and April 2026
Weak Q4 2025 GDP growth of 0.7% (second estimate) creates low base
GDPNow has average absolute error of 0.77 percentage points historically
Consumer spending and investment growth nowcasts both revised down
risks: GDPNow model could be overly pessimistic - other forecasts may be more accurate
Late-quarter economic data could significantly revise estimates upward
BEA methodology differences from nowcast models could produce higher reading
Seasonal adjustment factors might favor Q1 growth more than expected
Model accuracy decreases further from release date
